lesson.menu_desc: Consolidation of lessons 1 to 9 -- from first words to first interactions -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- Block 1 Review and Consolidation
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Eduardo | Aula 10 -- Block 1 Review and Consolidation

vocab.title: Match each Block 1 word to its meaning
defs: using nature and resources in a careful way for the future
an official paper that proves something is correct
a farmer or company that grows and sells the coffee
the rules or points you check in an audit
past of submit: you sent a document to someone official
past of approve: someone said yes, it is correct

paras: My name is Carlos and I am an analyst at Sucafina. Sucafina is a Swiss coffee company, and it is responsible for sustainability. I am from Virginia, a small town near Varginha, in the coffee region of southern Minas Gerais.

Every week, I check documents and I verify certificates. There are many coffee farms in my region, and there is a big warehouse next to the main road. Our office is in Varginha, and the headquarters is in Geneva.

In an online meeting, I can share my screen and I can present the audit criteria. If the connection is bad, I say: 'Could you repeat that, please?' Last week, I visited a farm. I completed the checklist and I found one problem: the producer did not have a valid certificate. I wrote a corrective action plan and I submitted the report. Emma approved two farms, but she did not approve the third one yet.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- A1 Block 1 review, all core structures

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Carlos explains how to make an espresso at home.
Carlos gives a full picture of his job at Sucafina, using everything from Block 1.
Carlos writes a complaint about a late coffee shipment.

items: Sucafina is a Swiss coffee company.
The text says: 'Sucafina is a Swiss coffee company.'
There are no coffee farms in Carlos's region.
The text says: 'There are many coffee farms in my region.'
Carlos can present the audit criteria in a meeting.
The text says: 'I can share my screen and I can present the audit criteria.'
Emma approved all three farms last week.
The text says: 'Emma approved two farms, but she did not approve the third one yet.'

parts: many farms in my region. I
a certificate for each farm. In a meeting I
share my screen. Last week I

gapfill.text: Block 1 in one picture: to be (I am), present simple (I check), there is / there are (There are many farms), can / could (I can share; Could you repeat?), and simple past (Last week I visited). Use the base verb after can and did not.

bank.label: Say your whole story with Block 1
items: I am an analyst at Sucafina.
There are many farms in my region.
I can present the audit criteria.
Could you send me the checklist, please?
Last week, I visited a farm.
I submitted the report on Tuesday.

lf.text: to be = am / is / are. there is / there are = places that exist. can / could = ability and requests. simple past = -ed (visited) or irregular (found, wrote) for last week.

items.situation: Introduce yourself: name, job, and company.
tips: I am Carlos, an analyst at Sucafina.
My name is Carlos. I work at Sucafina.
I verify certificates and I review reports.

items.situation: Describe your region (there is / there are).
tips: There are many farms in my region.
There is a warehouse next to the road.

items.situation: Make a polite request in a meeting (could).
tips: Could you send me the checklist, please?

items.situation: Report one thing you did last week (simple past).
tips: Last week, I visited a farm.
I submitted the report on Tuesday.

items: Introduce yourself and your job: name, company, region, and daily routine.
Use there is / there are and prepositions to describe your farms, office, and warehouse.
Open an online meeting: greet, share your screen, and make a request with could.
Report last week's field visit: what you visited, found, wrote, and submitted.

answerkey.title: Reveal the Block 1 summary
list: Who I am: 'I am Carlos, an analyst at Sucafina. Sucafina is a Swiss coffee company.'
My region: 'There are many farms in my region. There is a warehouse next to the road.'
In a meeting: 'I can present the criteria. Could you send me the checklist?'
Last week: 'I visited a farm, I found one problem, and I submitted the report.'
answerkey.note: Block 1 = to be + present simple + have/has + there is/are + prepositions + can/could + simple past.

listenings.text: Let me tell you about my work in one minute. My name is Carlos, and I am an analyst at Sucafina. Sucafina is a Swiss coffee company. I am from Virginia, a small town in the coffee region of southern Minas Gerais. Every week, I check documents and I verify certificates. There are many farms in my region, and there is a big warehouse next to the road. In an online meeting, I can share my screen and I can present the audit criteria. Last week, I visited a farm, I found one problem, and I submitted the report. This is my job, and now I can talk about it in English.

Hi Carlos, this is Emma. I want to say congratulations. Ten weeks ago, you said only a few words in English. Now you can introduce yourself, describe your region, run a meeting, and report a farm visit in the past. Could you send me your short text about your work at Sucafina? I want to read it before Block 2. In Block 2, we build longer sentences and we talk about future plans. You did great work. See you soon, and welcome to the next block.

extra_audio.text: First, Carlos introduces himself: I am an analyst at Sucafina. Then he describes his region: there are many farms near the town. In a meeting, he says: I can present the criteria. He makes a request: could you send me the checklist? Last week, he visited a farm and submitted the report.
